summary: Cleaned up and renamed many ftcommon helpers and related Fighter fields/attributes, replacing address-based names with behavior names for ground friction/movement, air friction, velocity clamping, falling/fast-falling, ECB unlock, jump consumption, facing update, and grab mash logic. The PR propagated those names across 104 fighter/common and character special files, updated symbols and Doxygen declarations, simplified duplicated helper bodies, and kept the GALE01 decomp.dev report at "No changes", indicating no binary output changes for the reported target.
what_changed: Renamed address-style ftcommon symbols in config/GALE01/symbols.txt, headers, Doxygen, implementations, and callsites; examples include ftCommon_8007C930 to ftCommon_ApplyFrictionGround, ftCommon_8007CB74 to ftCommon_ApplyGroundMovement, ftCommon_8007CC78 to ftCommon_ClampGrVel, ftCommon_8007CE94 to ftCommon_ApplyFrictionAir, ftCommon_8007D468 to ftCommon_ClampAirDrift, ftCommon_8007D494 to ftCommon_Fall, ftCommon_8007D4E4 to ftCommon_FallFast, ftCommon_8007D528 to ftCommon_CheckFallFast, ftCommon_8007D5BC to ftCommon_UnlockECB, ftCommon_8007D698 to ftCommon_UseAllJumps, ftCommon_8007D9FC to ftCommon_UpdateFacing, and ftCommon_8007DC08 to ftCommon_GrabMash.
Renamed Fighter bitfield x221A_b4 to fall_fast and updated resets, fall/air physics checks, Link down-air handling, and fast-fall detection to use the semantic field name.
Renamed ftCo_DatAttrs fields grounded_max_horizontal_velocity to ground_max_horizontal_velocity and horizontal_air_mobility_constant to air_max_horizontal_velocity.
Named ftCommonData warp-star drift parameters at offsets +69C/+6A0/+6A4 as warpstarfall_drift_scaling, warpstarfall_drift_flat, and warpstarfall_drift_max.
Improved function prototypes and local variables by using behavior-oriented parameter names such as accel, target_vel, friction, threshold, accel_max, target_max, gravity, terminal_vel, and max.

Simplified repeated helper implementations in ftcommon.c: ftCommon_ClampAirDrift delegates to ftCommon_ClampSelfVelX, ftCommon_FallBasic delegates to ftCommon_Fall, ftCommon_8007DA24 delegates facing updates to ftCommon_UpdateFacing, and ground transition helper code was reduced by calling ftCommon_UnlockECB and ftCommon_8007D6A4.
Replaced several magic ground/air comparisons with GA_Ground and GA_Air enum constants in ftcommon.c.

Propagated the new helper names through many common states and character special files, especially physics/collision code for fall, fall special, air catch, bury, capture states, dash/run/passive wall, warp star, and numerous specials.

Changed ftCo_Dash_Enter call argument from fp->mv.ca.specials.grav to fp->mv.co.dash.x0 in the diff excerpt; the context suggests this was part of cleanup/understanding, but the PR text does not explicitly explain it.
Updated src/melee/ft/ftcommon.dox to keep documentation declarations aligned with renamed functions.
smart_moves: Renamed functions by observable behavior and then updated every callsite, making future decomp work searchable by mechanics like friction, drift, falling, ECB, facing, and mashing instead of raw addresses.

Kept symbol map, header prototypes, Doxygen stubs, implementation names, and callsites synchronized, avoiding the common problem where only source references are renamed but tooling/documentation still exposes old names.

Used small wrapper/factoring cleanups that preserve behavior while expressing intent, such as ClampAirDrift calling ClampSelfVelX with air_drift_max and FallBasic calling Fall with gravity and terminal velocity.
Renamed the fast-fall bit from x221A_b4 to fall_fast at the struct definition and all observed uses, which is stronger than only naming the helper that checks it.
Replaced literal ground/air values with GA_Ground and GA_Air in touched code, improving readability without depending on uncertain new behavior.
Let decomp.dev validate the cleanup as producing no GALE01 changes; the bot comment explicitly reported "No changes".
decomp_lessons: When a helper’s role is understood from many callsites, rename the central helper and immediately propagate through character files so the semantic name becomes a project-wide convention.

For widely used physics helpers, parameter names are as important as function names: accel, target_vel, gravity, terminal_vel, friction, and max make callsite intent clearer and reduce future misreads.
If a field name is discovered through a helper, rename both the helper and the struct field together; ftCommon_CheckFallFast plus Fighter.fall_fast is much more useful than either change alone.
Safe cleanup PRs can still reduce future matching risk by factoring duplicate logic into already-matched helpers, provided the generated code report stays unchanged.
Update symbols.txt alongside C and headers for global renamed functions so decomp tooling and map-based searches share the same names.
Prefer existing enum names like GA_Ground/GA_Air over numeric literals when they do not perturb matching.
naming_conventions: Use ftCommon_<Verb><Object> names for understood common fighter helpers, e.g. ftCommon_ApplyFrictionGround, ftCommon_ApplyGroundMovement, ftCommon_ClampAirDrift, ftCommon_UpdateFacing.
Use explicit physics terminology: ground vs air, friction, movement, clamp, fall, ascend, drift, terminal velocity, fast fall.
Use boolean/bitfield names that read as state flags, e.g. fall_fast instead of x221A_b4.
Use attribute names that encode the domain and direction: ground_max_horizontal_velocity, air_max_horizontal_velocity, warpstarfall_drift_scaling, warpstarfall_drift_flat, warpstarfall_drift_max.
Keep address comments in headers while replacing the identifier with a semantic name, preserving both binary location and readability.
Use GA_Ground and GA_Air constants instead of raw 0/1 comparisons where ground_or_air semantics are known.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Renamed matched functions and variables without changing generated output; decomp.dev reported GALE01 "No changes".
Factored helper bodies in ways likely chosen to preserve codegen, such as delegating ClampAirDrift to ClampSelfVelX and FallBasic to Fall.
Removed redundant duplicated ground-transition code by calling an existing helper, while retaining matching according to the bot report.
Used semantic locals to clarify compiler-equivalent calculations, e.g. accel/target_vel instead of temporary phi variables.
Kept global symbol addresses and sizes stable while changing names in symbols.txt.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided slice.
The only issue comment was from decomp-dev[bot], reporting: "Report for GALE01 ... No changes".
The PR body notes the author was aware the broad rename cleanup could affect in-progress work: "sorry if this breaks any in-progress work..."
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that build on ftCommon_CheckFallFast or Fighter.fall_fast to identify remaining fast-fall cleanup.
Search for remaining ftCommon_8007D* address-named helpers near the renamed range to continue semantic naming.
Audit remaining uses of raw ground_or_air numeric comparisons and replace with GA_Ground or GA_Air where matching allows.
Investigate the ftCo_Dash_Enter argument change from fp->mv.ca.specials.grav to fp->mv.co.dash.x0 for whether it fixed a mistaken union-field access or only improved naming/readability.
Search for remaining ftCo_DatAttrs x-prefixed or misleading movement fields after ground_max_horizontal_velocity and air_max_horizontal_velocity.
